items.title: Mo Salah’s staggering 10-bus journey that took NINE HOURS every day to become Liverpool hero and Egypt’s World Cup star
items.summary: MOHAMED SALAH'S road to superstardom was anything but easy... literally. At just 14 years old, he would complete a gruelling NINE HOUR round trip using 10 different buses just so he could play football. items.summary: FOR most of the world, Iraq has too often been defined by conflict. items.title: Phil Foden’s heartbreak over Thomas Tuchel’s World Cup axe revealed by Man City teammate set to star for England instead
items.summary: NICO O’REILLY revealed Phil Foden was “gutted” to miss out on England’s World Cup squad. But Manchester City defender O’Reilly has backed his club-mate - and fellow snubbed star Cole Palmer - to show what they are made of next term. items.title: Hamilton claims spectacular first Grand Prix win for Ferrari
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton claimed his first race victory for Ferrari after an aggressive strategy paid off at the Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix as championship leader Kimi Antonelli dramatically retired late on.

items.title: Real Madrid ‘AGREE £51.8million deal to sign ex-Barcelona star Marc Cucurella from Chelsea’ in major blow to Xabi Alonso
items.summary: REAL MADRID have agreed a shock deal to sign Marc Cucurella from Chelsea. The Spanish left-back, 27, is set to move to the Bernabeu to join Jose Mourinho following the World Cup, on a deal worth £47.5million, rising to £51.8m with add-ons. items.title: Giroud: Pulisic reminded me of Hazard at Chelsea
items.summary: Olivier Giroud has said his former Chelsea and AC Milan teammate Christian Pulisic "reminded him of Eden Hazard."

items.title: Boyd: 'Nervy' Scotland must step it up against Morocco
items.summary: Kris Boyd claimed that Scotland looked 'nervous' as the favourites against Haiti and that they need to improve their performance levels if they want to secure a point against Morocco, which would all but see them qualify for the World Cup knockout stages.

items.title: FIFA: 'Technical outage' led to VAR controversy
items.summary: FIFA have confirmed that a "technical outage" meant that VAR's semi-automated offside technology was not able to be used for the controversial call that led to Switzerland's opening goal against Qatar on Saturday.

items.title: Sources: Somali ref to receive full World Cup pay
items.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan will be paid for his World Cup match assignments by FIFA, sources have told ESPN, despite being refused entry into the United States prior to the start of the tournament.
